sanskrit: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता । योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥
hindiTranslation: जैसे स्पन्दनरहित वायुके स्थानमें स्थित दीपककी लौ चेष्टारहित हो जाती है, योगका अभ्यास करते हुए वश में किए हुए चित्तवाले योगीके चित्तकी वैसी ही उपमा कही गयी है ॥
englishTranslation: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so the disciplined mind of a yogi does not waver while practicing yoga of the true self.
essence: A trained mind becomes steady enough to stop flickering.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A trained mind becomes steady and unmoving, like a lamp that does not flicker when there is no wind.
simpleInsight: Stillness is not emptiness; it is a mind no longer pulled around by every disturbance.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna keeps teaching Arjuna how meditation changes a person from the inside. After describing the controlled mind, he gives a vivid image: a lamp in a windless place. That is the calm he wants Arjuna to recognize.
modernRelevance: You finally sit down after a fight, but your thoughts keep replaying every word. This verse points to a steadiness that no argument, memory, or fear can shake.
translationLiteral: यथा (as) / दीपः (lamp) / निवातस्थः (placed in a windless place) / नेङ्गते (does not flicker) / सा (that) / उपमा (comparison) / स्मृता (is remembered) / योगिनः (of the yogi) / यतचित्तस्य (of the controlled mind) / युञ्जतः (practicing) / योगम् (yoga) / आत्मनः (of the true self)
insight: It shifts attention from managing thoughts to stabilizing the mind that holds them.

quoteVariants.text: A steady mind shines like a lamp without wind.
Your mind can hold still like a lamp in calm air.
Let the mind burn without flicker.
